title: UI Primitive Catalogue & Samantha Addressing Landscape
topic: Samantha UI primitive catalogue & addressing — survey of grid/table, tree, board, gantt, calendar, form, diagram, list, navigation, tabs, dialog, toast, palette across major SDKs (Rally, ExtJS, AG Grid, TanStack, MRT, Atlassian DS, Salesforce LWC, Fluent UI, Power Apps, monday/ClickUp/Asana, Retool, dnd-kit) and proposed samantha.ui._<kind>.<name>.… convention
summary: UI primitive survey across Rally, AG Grid, Atlassian, Salesforce; proposes samantha.ui._<kind>.<name> convention.
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Overview
  2. 2. Key Findings
  3. 3. Primitive Matrix
  4. 4. Library-by-Library Drill-down
  5. 5. Cross-cutting Patterns
  6. 6. Rank Persistence Strategies
  7. 7. Vector Mapping — proposed samantha.ui.* convention
  8. 8. What's On Disk vs. Net-new
  9. 9. Prioritisation Recommendation
  10. 10. Lint Rules & Frozen-Version Contract
  11. 11. Gaps
  12. 12. Sources

1. Overview

This paper surveys how leading enterprise UI SDKs (Rally App SDK 2.x, ExtJS, AG Grid, TanStack Table, Material React Table, Atlassian Design System, Salesforce Lightning, ServiceNow Now Experience, Microsoft Fluent UI/FAST, Power Apps, monday/ClickUp/Asana embedding APIs, Retool, dnd-kit, react-beautiful-dnd / Pragmatic DnD) expose structural primitives (grid, tree, board, gantt, calendar, form, diagram, list, navigation, tabs, dialog, toast, command-palette) and how they let custom-app authors address those primitives — the equivalent of Vector's samantha._viewport.<slot>._<kind>.<name> substrate. The goal is to ground a proposed samantha.ui._<kind>.<name>.… convention in real prior art and to identify which primitives Vector should build next.

2. Key Findings

  • Config-flag DnD is the enterprise-grid pattern. Rally's enableRanking: true, MUI X Pro's rowReordering, Material React Table's enableRowOrdering, and AG Grid's rowDragManaged all collapse drag-and-drop ordering into a single boolean. The grid then auto-mounts the plugin and (for Rally) auto-persists via Rally.data.Ranker.rankRelative.
  • "Managed" vs "unmanaged" is now standard vocabulary. AG Grid coined the split — managed mode auto-reorders client-side state; unmanaged mode emits events and the host owns the model. TanStack Table and dnd-kit are unmanaged-only by design.
  • react-beautiful-dnd is archived (Aug 2025). Atlassian's successor is Pragmatic DnD, which is HTML5-DnD-based and ships with the Atlassian Design System. @dnd-kit remains the React community default and is already Vector's chosen library.
  • TanStack is loud about not doing DnD. Both TanStack Table and TanStack Virtual delegate to @dnd-kit in their official examples. This is the deliberate "headless" stance: no opinionated UI primitives at all.
  • Two rank-string ecosystems dominate. Rally uses base-94 DragAndDropRank; Atlassian/Jira uses base-26 LexoRank. Both produce sparse, infinitely-rebalanceable strings. Generic "fractional indexing" (Figma, Notion, Linear) uses big-decimal midpoints with the same intent.
  • Addressability has three styles. (a) Per-element user-given IDs (Salesforce Lightning, Retool, Power Apps, monday) — the host page is a tree of named components and code references them by stable id. (b) Data-attribute conventions (Atlassian, Fluent UI, Radix) — primitives expose data-testid / data-rbd-* hooks. (c) None at all (TanStack, MRT, dnd-kit) — the integrator wires up their own addressing.
  • Frozen-version contracts are how shared SDKs avoid breakage. Rally pins to App SDK 2.x; Salesforce LWC components are versioned per API release; ServiceNow's Now Experience exposes versioned Web Components. Vector's samantha.diagram.canvas v1 already follows this pattern.
  • Board / kanban primitives are surprisingly rare as first-class SDK exports. monday, ClickUp, Asana surface them via embed APIs; Atlassian ships board components inside Jira but not in the public Design System. Most teams build them on top of @dnd-kit.
  • Gantt / timeline primitives are almost universally third-party. Bryntum, dhtmlx, Syncfusion dominate. Few platforms include one in their core SDK. Power Apps lists Gantt as a custom component starter, not a built-in.
  • Command palettes are the newest primitive. shadcn/ui's cmdk popularised it; Salesforce's "Quick Action" palette and ServiceNow's Now Assistant follow the same pattern. Atlassian Design System added one in 2024.

3. Primitive Matrix

Coverage of structural primitives across the surveyed libraries. "Built-in" = first-class SDK component. "Plugin" = official add-on. "DIY" = community/integrator builds it.

Primitive Rally 2.x ExtJS AG Grid Atlassian DS Salesforce LWC Fluent UI Power Apps Retool
Grid / TableBuilt-inBuilt-inBuilt-inBuilt-in (DynamicTable)Built-in (lightning-datatable)Built-in (DataGrid)Built-inBuilt-in
TreeBuilt-in (Tree)Built-inBuilt-in (Tree Data)PluginBuilt-in (lightning-tree)Built-in (Tree)DIYBuilt-in
Board / KanbanBuilt-in (CardBoard)DIYDIYDIYDIYDIYDIYBuilt-in
Gantt / TimelinePluginPlugin (Pro)DIYDIYDIYDIYDIYDIY
CalendarDIYBuilt-inDIYBuilt-inBuilt-inBuilt-in (Calendar)Built-inBuilt-in
FormBuilt-inBuilt-inDIYBuilt-inBuilt-in (lightning-record-edit-form)Built-inBuilt-inBuilt-in
Diagram / CanvasDIYPlugin (D3)DIYDIYDIYDIYDIYDIY
ListBuilt-inBuilt-inn/aBuilt-inBuilt-inBuilt-in (List)Built-inBuilt-in
NavigationBuilt-inBuilt-inn/aBuilt-in (SideNav)Built-inBuilt-in (Nav)Built-inBuilt-in
TabsBuilt-inBuilt-inn/aBuilt-inBuilt-inBuilt-in (Pivot)Built-inBuilt-in
Dialog / ModalBuilt-inBuilt-inn/aBuilt-in (Modal)Built-in (lightning-dialog)Built-inBuilt-inBuilt-in
Toast / NotificationBuilt-inBuilt-inn/aBuilt-in (Flag)Built-in (lightning-toast)Built-in (Toast)Built-inBuilt-in
Command Paletten/an/an/aBuilt-in (2024+)Built-in (Quick Action)Pluginn/an/a

4. Library-by-Library Drill-down

Rally App SDK 2.x

The reference enterprise pattern. Grid drag-rank is one boolean: enableRanking: true on a Rally.ui.grid.Grid auto-mounts Rally.ui.grid.plugin.DragDrop, which extends ExtJS Ext.grid.plugin.DragDrop. On drop, the grid calls Rally.data.Ranker.rankRelative(record, before, after) which writes the DragAndDropRank field (sparse base-94 string) back to WSAPI. The integrator never sees the persistence layer. Custom apps address this via Rally.ui.grid.Grid by config; there's no per-instance "name" — the grid is a JS object you keep a reference to.

ExtJS (Sencha)

Rally's substrate. Plugin-oriented: Ext.grid.plugin.DragDrop, Ext.tree.plugin.TreeViewDragDrop, etc. Components are addressable via Ext.getCmp('id') (per-component user-given id) or Ext.ComponentQuery.query('grid[name=foo]') (selector syntax). Forms a precedent for "per-element user id" addressability.

AG Grid

Coined the "managed vs unmanaged" split. rowDragManaged: true reorders the client-side row model and emits onRowDragEnd. Persistence is the integrator's job. Tree-data mode (treeData: true) supports drag-into-folder via onRowDragEnter/Move/End. No built-in addressability beyond the gridApi reference returned at mount.

TanStack Table

Headless. Provides table state (sorting, filtering, pagination, grouping) but renders nothing. DnD is explicitly out of scope — official examples wire @dnd-kit/sortable on top. No addressability primitive.

Material React Table (MRT)

TanStack Table + Material UI rendering. Adds enableRowOrdering and enableColumnOrdering as opt-in booleans. Persistence is the integrator's job. Internal DnD uses @dnd-kit.

Atlassian Design System

Tokens-first system. DynamicTable, SideNav, Modal, Flag (toast), Tabs, Form are first-class. Drag is delegated to Pragmatic DnD (the post-react-beautiful-dnd successor). Addressability is via standard React refs + data-testid; no global registry.

Salesforce Lightning Web Components (LWC)

Per-element user-given ids are first-class: every <lightning-input data-id="foo"> is queryable via this.template.querySelector('[data-id="foo"]'). The Lightning Locker / Aura framework registers components in a tree the platform can introspect. Closest analogue to Vector's named-addressable substrate.

ServiceNow Now Experience

Web Components published as now-* custom elements. Each takes attributes; addressability is by-DOM. Versioned per Now Platform release. Drag is part of the data-table component.

Microsoft Fluent UI / FAST

Tokens + components for both React (Fluent UI v9) and standalone Web Components (FAST). Built-in DataGrid, Tree, Calendar, Toast, Nav. DnD is integrator-supplied. Addressability via React keys / Web Component slot ids.

Power Apps

Each control on a screen has a designer-given name (e.g. Gallery1) which is the addressable handle. Power FX expressions reference Gallery1.Selected. This is a strong precedent for "the user names the element and the platform persists the name".

monday / ClickUp / Asana

Embedding APIs rather than UI SDKs. monday's monday-sdk-js exposes board context to embedded views; ClickUp's app framework is similar. None ship reusable React primitives — the apps are custom UI talking to a host.

Retool

Designer-named components like Power Apps. table1, form1, etc. Drag-rank is a built-in column type on tables (handleColumn) with no persistence wiring — the integrator writes the SQL.

dnd-kit

The current React DnD default. Headless: useDraggable, useDroppable, SortableContext. No primitives at all — pure mechanics. Vector has already adopted this (see source 14).

react-beautiful-dnd

Archived August 2025. Successor is Atlassian's Pragmatic DnD (HTML5-DnD-based, smaller bundle). Vector should NOT pick this up.

5. Cross-cutting Patterns

  • One-flag DnD on data primitives. Rally enableRanking, AG Grid rowDragManaged, MRT enableRowOrdering, MUI X Pro rowReordering. The flag promises auto-persistence (Rally) or at least auto-reorder (AG Grid managed mode).
  • Plugin sub-namespace for DnD. Rally Rally.ui.grid.plugin.DragDrop, ExtJS Ext.grid.plugin.DragDrop, ExtJS Ext.tree.plugin.TreeViewDragDrop. The plugin lives under the primitive's namespace, not as a sibling.
  • Designer-named addressability is the enterprise norm. Salesforce, Power Apps, Retool, monday all let the page author name a component; the platform persists the name and exposes it to scripts.
  • Server is order-of-truth. Rally writes DragAndDropRank on every drop; Jira writes customfield_* LexoRank; Asana writes insert_before/after. None trust the client to keep order.
  • Frozen-version contracts protect custom-app authors. Rally App SDK 2.x has been stable since 2014; Salesforce LWC API versions are pinned per release.

6. Rank Persistence Strategies

StrategyUsed byField shapeRebalance costInsert cost
Base-94 sparse string (DragAndDropRank)Rally / CA Agile CentralString, ~12 charsRare; lazyO(1) — midpoint
LexoRank (base-26 sparse string)Atlassian / JiraString, 8–10 chars typicalPeriodic batchO(1) — midpoint
Fractional indexing (big-decimal)Figma, Notion, LinearDecimal / stringNever (in theory)O(1) — midpoint
Linked-list pointersAsana insert_before, Trello (legacy)Two FK columnsn/aO(1) — pointer swap
Integer "position" columnMany homegrown systemsintO(N) on insert mid-listO(N) — shift others

Vector's existing rank discipline (see docs/c_c_ranking.md) uses the integer-position model with a NOTIFY trigger for realtime adoption. For high-frequency reordering at scale, base-94 / LexoRank-style sparse strings are the proven path.

7. Vector Mapping — proposed samantha.ui.* convention

Vector's existing addressable substrate (samantha._viewport.<slot>._<kind>.<name>, see docs/c_c_addressables.md) already covers _panel, _table, _navigation, _header, _tree. This proposal extends the same scheme to a full UI primitive catalogue, adds plugin sub-addresses for cross-cutting features (DnD, filters, exports), and pins each primitive to a frozen-version contract.

7.1 Proposed kinds

Kind tokenPurposeExisting Vector componentStatus
_tableTabular data, sortable/filterable/pageable rowsapp/components/Table.tsxon disk
_treeHierarchical resource browserapp/components/ResourceTree.tsxon disk
_diagramCanvas2D node-and-edgeapp/components/diagram-canvas/on disk (v1 frozen)
_timeboxSprint / release / future kindsapp/components/timebox/on disk
_boardKanban columns of cards, drag-between—net-new
_ganttTimeline of dated bars—net-new
_calendarMonth/week/day views—net-new
_formField collection with validationpartial (per-page bespoke)net-new (consolidate)
_listVertical row stack, drag-rank optionalpartial (per-page bespoke)net-new (consolidate)
_navigationPrimary / side navexisting addressable adopteron disk
_tabsSecondary nav inside a pagepartial (per-page bespoke)net-new (consolidate)
_dialogModal overlaypartialnet-new (consolidate)
_toastTransient notificationexisting viewport sloton disk (slot only)
_paletteCommand palette / quick-action—net-new

7.2 Plugin sub-addresses

Mirroring Rally's Rally.ui.grid.plugin.DragDrop, plugins live under the primitive instance:

samantha.ui._table.<name>._dnd          # row drag-rank
samantha.ui._table.<name>._filter       # filter chips above grid
samantha.ui._table.<name>._export       # CSV / XLSX
samantha.ui._table.<name>._inline_edit  # cell editor
samantha.ui._tree.<name>._dnd           # node drag-into-folder
samantha.ui._board.<name>._dnd          # card drag-between-columns
samantha.ui._list.<name>._dnd           # row reordering

Each plugin sub-address is its own addressable row (registered alongside its parent) so help-defaults, RBAC, and lint can target it independently.

7.3 Server-persistence contract

For any _dnd sub-address, the contract is:

  1. Frontend emits onRankChange(record, before, after) via the addressable's standard event hook.
  2. The address resolves to a Go writer-boundary service registered for that _dnd address (one writer per orderable resource — see docs/c_c_ranking.md).
  3. Service computes new position (current Vector convention) or sparse-string rank (future) and writes inside a transaction.
  4. Service emits NOTIFY for realtime fan-out.
  5. Lint rule lint:writer-boundary blocks any handler outside the registered writer from mutating ranked rows.

7.4 Frozen-version contract pattern

Mirror samantha.diagram.canvas v1:

samantha.ui._table v1 {
  props: {                        // frozen prop set
    columns:    Column[]          // shape: { id, header, accessor, render? }
    rows:       Row[]
    onRankChange?: (r,b,a) => void
    plugins?:   PluginConfig
  }
  events: {                       // frozen event set
    rowClick, rowDoubleClick, rankChange, filterChange
  }
  plugins: {
    _dnd, _filter, _export, _inline_edit
  }
}

Breaking changes require samantha.ui._table v2; v1 stays mounted and addressable until all consumers migrate. Custom-app authors pin to a version.

8. What's On Disk vs. Net-new

Inventory of the components folder and adjacent registries:

PrimitiveFileAddress kindDnD plugin?
<Table>app/components/Table.tsx_tablepartial — drag-handle column exists (DragHandleColumn.tsx) but no _dnd sub-address registered
<ResourceTree>app/components/ResourceTree.tsx_treeno — read-only today
<DiagramCanvas>app/components/diagram-canvas/samantha.diagram.canvas v1 (legacy namespace)n/a — node-drag is intrinsic to canvas, not a plugin
<TimeboxManager>app/components/timebox/_timebox.<kind>.<name>no
<Badge>app/components/Badge.tsxn/a (atom, not addressable)n/a
<BulkActionBar>app/components/BulkActionBar.tsxnot yet addressablen/a
<DragHandleColumn>app/components/DragHandleColumn.tsxhelper for _table—

Net-new primitives Vector does not yet have: _board, _gantt, _calendar, _palette. Consolidate bespoke usage into a sanctioned primitive: _form, _list, _tabs, _dialog.

9. Prioritisation Recommendation

  1. P1 — _table._dnd (Rally parity). Single boolean prop on <Table>, auto-mounts the drag-handle column, auto-wires onRankChange to the registered writer. This is the highest-leverage primitive — every table with a "position" column today does ad-hoc DnD work. Reuses existing DragHandleColumn.tsx.
  2. P2 — _tree._dnd. Drag-into-folder for <ResourceTree>. Required for the work-items hierarchy and the org-topology canvas siblings. Spec the disallowed-move predicate (no cycles, no cross-tenant).
  3. P3 — _board primitive. Kanban cards/columns. Highest-visibility net-new. Build on @dnd-kit with _dnd sub-address from day one. Useful for both backlog and pipeline-style views.
  4. P4 — _form consolidation. One sanctioned form primitive. Replaces a long tail of bespoke per-page forms. Should expose _validation and _autosave sub-addresses.
  5. P5 — _palette primitive. Vector lacks a Cmd-K. With addressables already in place, every adopted element is a navigation target — the palette is mostly UI on top of the existing registry.
  6. P6 — _calendar / _gantt. Defer until a concrete feature needs them. Use Bryntum or a community library on first need; do not roll our own without a forcing function.

10. Lint Rules & Frozen-Version Contract

Mirror existing project lint discipline (lint:addressables, lint:role-literals, lint:writer-boundary, lint:dev-css):

  • lint:samantha-ui-kind — every samantha.ui._<kind> address must use a kind from the allow-list above; freeform kinds rejected. Exemption ledger at dev/registries/samantha-ui-kinds.json.
  • lint:samantha-ui-plugin — every _dnd / _filter / _export sub-address must be registered alongside a parent address; orphan plugins rejected.
  • lint:samantha-ui-version — every primitive component file must export a SAMANTHA_VERSION constant; bumping it without a deprecation entry triggers a build failure.
  • Extend lint:writer-boundary — for _dnd addresses, the writer must be a registered orderable-resource service from docs/c_c_ranking.md; ad-hoc UPDATE … SET position in handlers rejected.

11. Gaps

  • Atlassian's Pragmatic DnD migration trade-offs vs @dnd-kit were not researched in depth — Vector has already chosen @dnd-kit, so this is academic, but worth a follow-up if bundle size becomes a constraint.
  • Salesforce LWC's per-element-id mechanism uses platform-level component metadata that's not directly transferable; the parallel to Vector's address registry is conceptual, not literal.
  • Power Apps Power FX expression syntax was not surveyed; Vector's equivalent (custom-app expression language for Samantha consumers) is out of scope here.
  • No survey of mobile-native UI SDKs (UIKit/SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose) — out of scope for the web addressable substrate but relevant if Vector ships native shells.
  • No empirical performance comparison of the rank-string strategies under heavy concurrent reordering; literature is qualitative.
